title: Westfield man busted in alleged Red Bull SNAP scam at Chicopee smoke shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mohamed Yahiya, 47, was arrested in Chicopee for allegedly running a SNAP and EBT fraud scheme involving Red Bull.
Authorities say Yahiya directed SNAP users to buy bulk Red Bull, then bought it back for cash at a discount.
Yahiya faces multiple charges, including food stamp trafficking, selling unstamped cigarettes, and possession with intent to distribute marijuana.

title: Free speech rally planned outside BU president’s office amid Pride flag dispute
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rally planned at BU to address Pride flag removal, organized by Terrier Courage, occurs Thursday at 1 Silber Way.
Petition against BU's signage policy reached 90% of 2,000 signatures, seeks allowances for flags in private spaces.
BU's current policy restricts signage in outward-facing windows, leading to flag removal, sparking free speech debate.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in April
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Mark Wahlberg stars in 'Balls Up' on Prime Video, streaming April 15, directed by Peter Farrelly.
Hulu's 'The Testaments,' set in dystopian Boston, premieres April 8 as a follow-up to 'The Handmaid's Tale'.
HBO Max releases the fifth season of 'Hacks' on April 9 and 'Euphoria' season 3 on April 13.

title: What to know about the MBTA’s free and discounted commuter rail plans for summer
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA is offering free commuter rail rides on Fridays from June to August, except for special event trains.
Monthly commuter rail passes will be 50% off for summer, excluding Zone 1A and non-monthly tickets.
Weekend passholders can travel to any zone and bring a companion for $1 each way, with exclusions.

title: Weston native Tim Demirjian wins Hulu’s ‘Love Overboard’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Weston native Tim Demirjian won Hulu's 'Love Overboard' with Gia Aldisert, claiming a trip around the world as their prize.
The series, hosted by Gabby Windey, follows singles on a yacht in Malta seeking love and avoiding elimination.
Produced by Alex Cooper, the show features contestants living luxuriously if coupled, or facing tasks if single.

title: MBTA to release train tickets for World Cup matches at Boston Stadium
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA will release train tickets for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium starting April 8 via the mTicket app.
Fans need a World Cup match ticket to purchase train tickets, which are nonrefundable and non-transferable.
Round-trip train fares to Gillette Stadium may exceed $75, compared to $30 for previous matches.

title: 2 Boston bars earn spots on 50 Best’s top 100 U.S. bars list
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Two Boston bars, The Wig Shop and Hecate, made the 50 Best's top 100 U.S. bars list.
The Wig Shop, ranked No. 86, features a wig-themed menu and is located at 27 Temple Place.
Hecate, at 48 Gloucester St., ranked No. 88, offers cocktails inspired by Greek mythology and a mystical theme.

title: Mass. man sentenced for online threats to public figures, children, elementary school
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Funwayo Mbilini Nyawo, 37, of Wilbraham, sentenced to 32 months for threatening posts targeting officials, children, and a school.
Nyawo pleaded guilty to 13 counts of threatening communications and one count of stalking using interstate commerce.
Threats included posts on X and Google against police, a Springfield school, and urged violence by terrorist organizations.

title: The Liberty Hotel unveils a $75K World Cup ultra-luxe package
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Liberty Hotel in Boston offers a $75,000 World Cup package with a presidential suite and match tickets.
The package includes helicopter transfers, premium seats, autographed jerseys, and a dedicated concierge service.
Guests must stay at least three nights, booking at least 4-5 weeks in advance for June and July match dates.

title: Umpire admits he forgot the count during bizarre Red Sox sequence
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Umpire Mark Wegner admitted he forgot the strike count during a Red Sox vs. Astros game on Tuesday night.
Red Sox catcher Conor Wong's errant throw allowed an Astros runner to score, compounding the umpire's counting error.
Houston's Cam Smith unknowingly benefited from the error, ultimately reaching base with a walk after swinging out.
